issues.body: The module states that colluding parties' views are independent of every key and that the result is the only revealed information. Each comparison/equality invokes Rabbit decomposition, which opens c=(x+r) mod p; the existing comparator documentation characterizes this as near-uniform with a nonzero statistical leakage floor, not information-theoretic independence. Repeating these openings throughout O(n log^2 n) comparisons also requires accounting for composition. The sq-qhy4 caveat does not make the categorical claim honest.
issues.fix_hint: Replace the independence/result-only language with a precise statistically-hiding claim, state the per-opening bound and its composition over the modeled number of decompositions, and retain the semi-honest/not-externally-audited caveat. Update matching public docs in the same change.
issues.title: Privacy claim contradicts the statistical Rabbit masking model

issues.body: randomised_differential_semi_and_anti does not generate randomized inputs: it runs six fixed, small layouts and reuses one seed per layout/party count. It therefore provides weak coverage of arbitrary network widths, group boundaries, duplicate patterns, and comparator-range edges. There is also no mutation test demonstrating that the oracle harness fails when the new sort/scan produces a deliberately wrong answer. For a new correctness-critical fast path, the supplied test evidence is insufficient.
issues.fix_hint: Add a deterministic property-style differential over many generated lengths, seeds, key multisets, duplicates, empty sides, and values near the supported upper bound, exercising both Semi and Anti. Add a mutation check (or an equivalent test-only fault injection) that demonstrably turns the differential red when a selector, adjacency equality, scan direction, or swap decision is corrupted.
issues.title: Novel sort-merge path lacks a real randomized differential and mutation check

verdict.summary: The implementation has plausible functional coverage for small examples, but this soundness-critical MPC change is not ready to approve. Its privacy documentation overstates information-theoretic independence despite the Rabbit masked openings being only statistically hiding, and the purported randomized differential test is a short fixed case table with no mutation check. Those gaps directly conflict with the repository's live MPC honesty gate and novel-algorithm verification requirements.
